_description: Starter parameters for revenue-forecaster. All values are neutral defaults — copy this file, rename to parameters.json, and tune with autoresearch on your own historical data.

blending._note: Weights should sum to 1.0. Recent captures current volume, seasonal anchors to same-month history, YoY adds growth.

blending_no_yoy._note: Used when an entity has no prior-year data (new business, < 12 months of history).

exponential_weighting._note: 1.0 = equal weight across recent weeks. < 1.0 = more weight on most-recent weeks. Use 0.95 for fast-changing volume; 1.0 for stable.

outlier_handling._note: Caps any week at threshold_pct * rolling_median to prevent holiday weeks from inflating the recent average.

week_of_month._note: Some businesses have systematic within-month patterns (pay-week spikes, rent-week dips). Start disabled; enable and tune per-entity if autoresearch finds wins here.

holidays._note: Multiplicative: 1.0 = no effect, 2.0 = double, 0.5 = half. Start at 1.0 for all holidays and let autoresearch discover which matter for your business. Pre/post variants handle lead-up and hangover effects.

growth_adjustment._note: Number of recent months used to compute the YoY growth rate. Longer = smoother, shorter = more reactive.

data_filters._note: Exclude weeks before data_start_date or below min_weekly_revenue. Useful for excluding opening-ramp weeks that would distort the seasonal baseline.

per_entity._note: Override any field above per entity. Per-entity values win field-by-field. Example: { "Main Street": { "holidays": { "christmas_mult": 2.05 } } } keeps all global holidays except christmas_mult for Main Street.
